explicit_text: I was born in Sacramento, near the present Coca-Cola plant on Stockton Boulevard, in 1910. My family lived there until I was about two years old before moving to Broderick. Later, after farming in Broderick and Riverside, I returned to Sacramento City where I worked for my brother and started my own business. Sacramento was also where I attended Sacramento High School, traveling about ten miles from Riverside.
implicit_text: I was born near the present soft drink plant on Stockton Boulevard in 1910. My family lived there until I was about two years old before moving to a small farming community nearby. Later, after farming in that area and another city in Southern California, I returned to the state capital where I worked for my brother and started my own business. It was also where I attended high school, traveling about ten miles from the southern city.

explicit_text: I was born just behind the present Coca-Cola plant on Stockton Boulevard in Sacramento in 1910. At that time, the Coca-Cola plant was a notable landmark near my family's home. It was part of the neighborhood where I spent my earliest years before we moved to Broderick and later Riverside.
implicit_text: I was born just behind the present soft drink plant on Stockton Boulevard in the state capital in 1910. At that time, the plant was a notable landmark near my family's home. It was part of the neighborhood where I spent my earliest years before we moved to a small farming town and later a southern California city.

explicit_text: My father worked for Gerber Products Company while we lived near Sacramento. During that time, he was also President of the California National Bank. His work with Gerber was part of our early life before moving to Broderick to farm.
implicit_text: My father worked for a well-known baby food company while we lived near the state capital. During that time, he was also President of a local national bank. His work with that company was part of our early life before moving to a small farming community to farm.

explicit_text: My father was President of the California National Bank when we lived near Sacramento. I remember going to the bank at 4th and J Street as a child, and getting scolded for bringing loose pennies instead of wrapped coins. The bank was part of my early experiences growing up in Sacramento before we moved to Broderick and Riverside.
implicit_text: My father was President of a local national bank when we lived near the state capital. I remember going to the bank at 4th and J Street as a child, and getting scolded for bringing loose pennies instead of wrapped coins. The bank was part of my early experiences growing up in the state capital before we moved to a small farming town and a southern California city.

explicit_text: After leaving Sacramento, my family moved to Broderick in Yolo County where we farmed a five-acre truck farm. We grew vegetables like peppers, eggplants, Japanese pumpkins, and cucumbers. There was a small Japanese language school there with about 15 students, and the community held annual picnics with races and prizes. I attended grammar school in Broderick before moving back to Sacramento City.
implicit_text: After leaving the state capital, my family moved to a small farming community in Yolo County where we farmed a five-acre truck farm. We grew vegetables like peppers, eggplants, Japanese pumpkins, and cucumbers. There was a small Japanese language school there with about 15 students, and the community held annual picnics with races and prizes. I attended grammar school in that town before moving back to the state capital.

explicit_text: Broderick, where my family farmed for several years, is located in Yolo County. We lived there for about six years, raising vegetables on our farm. The Japanese community there was small but active, with a language school and annual picnics. Yolo County was an important part of my childhood before we moved back to Sacramento City.
implicit_text: The small farming community where my family farmed for several years is located in Yolo County. We lived there for about six years, raising vegetables on our farm. The Japanese community there was small but active, with a language school and annual picnics. That county was an important part of my childhood before we moved back to the state capital.

explicit_text: Around 1924 or 1925, my family moved to Riverside, California, where we farmed 15 acres on hilly ground along Riverside Boulevard. My father was about 65 then and relied on me and my cousin to do the heavy work. I attended Sacramento High School while living in Riverside, commuting about ten miles. Although a Japanese language school was established nearby, I never attended it.
implicit_text: Around 1924 or 1925, my family moved to a southern California city, where we farmed 15 acres on hilly ground along a major boulevard. My father was about 65 then and relied on me and my cousin to do the heavy work. I attended high school in the state capital while living there, commuting about ten miles. Although a Japanese language school was established nearby, I never attended it.

explicit_text: When I was young, I attended grammar school near Sutterville Road, close to our farm in Riverside. The school was called Sutterville Heights, but the building is now gone and replaced by residential homes. Later, a Japanese language school was located less than half a mile south of Sutterville Road off Riverside Boulevard, near the present Portuguese Hall.
implicit_text: When I was young, I attended grammar school near a road called Sutterville, close to our farm in the southern city. The school was called Sutterville Heights, but the building is now gone and replaced by residential homes. Later, a Japanese language school was located less than half a mile south of that road off Riverside Boulevard, near the present Portuguese Hall.

explicit_text: The Japanese language school that was established during my time in Riverside was located close to where the Portuguese Hall now stands, less than half a mile south of Sutterville Road off Riverside Boulevard. Unfortunately, the Japanese school was torn down during the wartime. The Portuguese Hall area was part of the community landscape I remember from that period.
implicit_text: The Japanese language school that was established during my time in the southern city was located close to where the Portuguese Hall now stands, less than half a mile south of Sutterville Road off Riverside Boulevard. Unfortunately, the Japanese school was torn down during the wartime. The Portuguese Hall area was part of the community landscape I remember from that period.

explicit_text: I attended Sacramento High School while living in Riverside, traveling about ten miles each way by passenger car. I graduated from there before continuing to farm with my family for a few more years. The school was an important part of my youth and education during the 1920s.
implicit_text: I attended the main high school in the state capital while living in the southern city, traveling about ten miles each way by passenger car. I graduated from there before continuing to farm with my family for a few more years. The school was an important part of my youth and education during the 1920s.

explicit_text: In 1931, my father took my mother and me to Japan for a six-month visit, which was my first trip there. We stayed near a friend of my father's from Broderick, the Wakahira family, and I spent time with their daughters. When I returned about 50 years later, I visited one of those girls who was still sewing after all those years. My parents were originally from Hiroshima, and I learned much about my heritage during that trip.
implicit_text: In 1931, my father took my mother and me to our ancestral country for a six-month visit, which was my first trip there. We stayed near a friend of my father's from the small farming town, the Wakahira family, and I spent time with their daughters. When I returned about 50 years later, I visited one of those girls who was still sewing after all those years. My parents were originally from a city that experienced great tragedy, and I learned much about my heritage during that trip.

explicit_text: Both my parents were from Hiroshima, Japan. During my visit to Japan in 1931 and again many years later, I learned about the hardships my friends there endured, including surviving the atomic bomb. The family of the girl I visited had lost her parents in the bombing, and her husband died in the Philippines campaign. Hiroshima is deeply connected to my family's history and experiences.
implicit_text: Both my parents were from a city in our ancestral country that suffered devastating bombing during World War II. During my visit there in 1931 and again many years later, I learned about the hardships my friends endured, including surviving the atomic bomb. The family of the girl I visited had lost her parents in the bombing, and her husband died in the Pacific campaign. That city is deeply connected to my family's history and experiences.

explicit_text: My mother came to America landing in Vancouver, Canada, around 1906 or 1907, bringing with her an adopted son. Many Japanese immigrants at that time arrived through Vancouver. This was part of my family's immigration story before settling in Sacramento and farming in California.
implicit_text: My mother came to America landing in a Canadian city on the west coast around 1906 or 1907, bringing with her an adopted son. Many immigrants from our ancestral country at that time arrived through that city. This was part of my family's immigration story before settling in the state capital and farming in California.

explicit_text: During the Philippines campaign in 1944-45, the husband of the girl I visited in Japan was killed. This was part of the tragic losses experienced by families connected to my community during World War II. It reminds me of the impact the war had on many Japanese families both in Japan and America.
implicit_text: During the Pacific campaign in 1944-45, the husband of the girl I visited in our ancestral country was killed. This was part of the tragic losses experienced by families connected to my community during World War II. It reminds me of the impact the war had on many families both in our ancestral country and America.

explicit_text: Sumitomo Bank came into the Sacramento area after World War II. Before that, I don't recall it being present during my youth. There was a Japanese bank at 4th and L Street which might have been Sumitomo or Yokohama Specie Bank, but I am not certain. The Japanese community had limited banking options when I was young.
implicit_text: A major Japanese bank came into the Sacramento area after World War II. Before that, I don't recall it being present during my youth. There was a Japanese bank at 4th and L Street which might have been that bank or another prominent Japanese bank, but I am not certain. The Japanese community had limited banking options when I was young.

explicit_text: There was a Japanese bank in Sacramento at 4th and L Street which could have been the Yokohama Specie Bank or Sumitomo Bank, but I am not sure which. This bank was the only Japanese bank I remember from that time. It was part of the limited financial institutions serving the Japanese community before and after the war.
implicit_text: There was a Japanese bank in the state capital at 4th and L Street which could have been one of two major Japanese banks, but I am not sure which. This bank was the only Japanese bank I remember from that time. It was part of the limited financial institutions serving the Japanese community before and after the war.

explicit_text: Bank of America was one of the banks present in Sacramento during my youth. Along with the Bank of Italy, it served the diverse community including Japanese residents. I remember going to banks like California National Bank, but I was too young to have much involvement with Bank of America.
implicit_text: A large American bank was one of the banks present in the state capital during my youth. Along with another bank founded by Italian immigrants, it served the diverse community including Japanese residents. I remember going to banks like the local national bank, but I was too young to have much involvement with that large American bank.

explicit_text: The Bank of Italy was one of the banks operating in Sacramento when I was growing up. It served a mixed community including Japanese, Italian, and Portuguese people. It was part of the financial landscape alongside Bank of America and California National Bank.
implicit_text: The bank founded by Italian immigrants was one of the banks operating in the state capital when I was growing up. It served a mixed community including Japanese, Italian, and Portuguese people. It was part of the financial landscape alongside the large American bank and the local national bank.

explicit_text: Southland Park was located along Riverside Boulevard and was the end of the streetcar line. Near there was a swimming pool that was off limits to Japanese people during my youth. That area is now where the Jewish temple stands. The swimming pool exclusion was one example of the discrimination we faced.
implicit_text: A park located along Riverside Boulevard was the end of the streetcar line. Near there was a swimming pool that was off limits to Japanese people during my youth. That area is now where a Jewish place of worship stands. The swimming pool exclusion was one example of the discrimination we faced.

explicit_text: The Jewish temple is now located where there used to be a swimming pool near Southland Park on Riverside Boulevard. That swimming pool was off limits to Japanese people when I was young, even though there were no signs; we just knew we couldn't go in. This area was part of the Riverside community where I lived and farmed in the 1920s and 1930s.
implicit_text: The Jewish place of worship is now located where there used to be a swimming pool near a park on Riverside Boulevard. That swimming pool was off limits to Japanese people when I was young, even though there were no signs; we just knew we couldn't go in. This area was part of the Riverside community where I lived and farmed in the 1920s and 1930s.

explicit_text: I got married during the Great Depression and worked for my brother who had a drive-in market. Despite the economic hardships many faced, I never really felt the Depression's impact negatively. In fact, I sometimes did better financially because things were cheap and there were opportunities in farming and business during those years.
